Patch Set 5:

>The other question here is how much this will affect real-world usage?  >Does node shutdown retry unmounting busy filesystems, or does this mean >that shutdown will be aborted and/or the node hard rebooted due to the >busy filesystem?

That is a good question which the answer will provide on how to solve this. So from my understanding the reason for the busy is that the portal layer still has active queues while unmounting. Upstream work was done to spin up and shutdown ptlrpc threads when mounting and unmounting. Would that address this issue? Neil any suggestions on that front.

Patch Set 5:

I was hoping that the changes in LU-12514 would resolve this problem without updating the config-sanity test. The reason for this issue is that ptlrpc message are still coming while umounting. With LU-12514 ptlrpc is brought down first. Issues never quit got that LU-12514 working correctly.

Topic set to upstream

parents.subject: LU-16518 mdt: fix unused variable compiler warnings

commit.subject: LU-10824 llite: remove may_umount() loop
commit.message:
LU-10824 llite: remove may_umount() loop

Al Viro pointed out the work around for LU-1882 is incorrect. It
can even lead to kernel panics under the right conditions. The
reason for this work around is that PtlRPC messages can still be
received after unmounting. With todays util-linux you can provide
umount helper scripts. Create one for Lustre that does the same
thing that the kernel tried to accomplish.

Fixes: 205a57df2787 ("LU-1882 llite: Adding timed wait in ll_umount_begin")
Test-Parameters: trivial testlist=conf-sanity env=ONLY=45,ONLY_REPEAT=100
Change-Id: I1498c6348022bdd6a74db887ffc8836a7a03bab6
Signed-off-by: Rick Mohr <rmohr@ornl.gov>
Signed-off-by: James Simmons <jsimmons@infradead.org>
